[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/bd/PC18_D7_SB-SL_Simon_23.jpg/250px-PC18_D7_SB-SL_Simon_23.jpg)](/wiki/File:PC18_D7_SB-SL_Simon_23.jpg)

[Simon Patmore](/wiki/Simon_Patmore "Simon Patmore") competing in a [banked slalom event](/wiki/Snowboarding_at_the_2018_Winter_Paralympics_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_banked_slalom "Snowboarding at the 2018 Winter Paralympics – Men's banked slalom") at the [2018 Paralympics](/wiki/2018_Paralympics "2018 Paralympics")

The **[Snowboarding](/wiki/Snowboarding "Snowboarding")** competition of the **[2018 Winter Paralympics](/wiki/2018_Winter_Paralympics "2018 Winter Paralympics")** was held on 12 and 16 March 2018 at the [Jeongseon Alpine Centre](/wiki/Jeongseon_Alpine_Centre "Jeongseon Alpine Centre") in [Gangneung](/wiki/Gangneung "Gangneung"), [South Korea](/wiki/South_Korea "South Korea").[[1]](#cite_note-Schedule-1)

## Classification

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Snowboarding_at_the_2018_Winter_Paralympics&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Classification")]

Main article: [Para-snowboarding classification](/wiki/Para-snowboarding_classification "Para-snowboarding classification")

Para-snowboarding is divided into three classification categories.[[2]](#cite_note-IPC_classification-2)

SB-LL1
:   Athletes competing in the class SB-LL1 have significant impairment to one leg, such as [amputation](/wiki/Amputation "Amputation") above the knee, or "a significant combined impairment in two legs", affecting their balance, their board-control and their ability to navigate uneven terrain.[[2]](#cite_note-IPC_classification-2) Athletes with annotations will use [prostheses](/wiki/Prostheses "Prostheses") during racing.[[2]](#cite_note-IPC_classification-2)

SB-LL2
:   Athletes competing in the class SB-LL2 have impairment to one or both legs "with less activity limitation", such as below-knee amputation.[[2]](#cite_note-IPC_classification-2)

SB-UL
:   Athletes competing in the class SB-UL have upper limb impairments, affecting balance.[[2]](#cite_note-IPC_classification-2) There are no events for female athletes in the category SB-UL in the 2018 games.[[3]](#cite_note-PyeongChang_snowboard_index-3)

## Competition schedule

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Snowboarding_at_the_2018_Winter_Paralympics&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Competition schedule")]

The following is the competition schedule for all 10 events (five categories in [snowboard cross](/wiki/Snowboard_cross "Snowboard cross") and five in [banked slalom](/wiki/Banked_slalom "Banked slalom")).[[1]](#cite_note-Schedule-1)

All times are local ([UTC+9](/wiki/Korea_Standard_Time "Korea Standard Time")).

| Date | Time | Event |
| --- | --- | --- |

| 10:42 | **Men's snowboard cross qualification run 1** |
| 11:54 | **Women's snowboard cross qualification run 2** |

## Medal summary

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Snowboarding_at_the_2018_Winter_Paralympics&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Medal summary")]

### Medal table

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Snowboarding_at_the_2018_Winter_Paralympics&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Medal table")]

The ranking in the table is based on information provided by the [International Paralympic Committee](/wiki/International_Paralympic_Committee "International Paralympic Committee") (IPC) and will be consistent with IPC convention in its published medal tables. By default, the table will be ordered by the number of gold medals the athletes from a nation have won (in this context, a "nation" is an entity represented by a National Paralympic Committee). The number of silver medals is taken into consideration next and then the number of bronze medals. If nations are still tied, equal ranking is given and they are listed alphabetically by IPC country code.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

| Rank | Nation | Gold | Silver | Bronze | Total |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | [United States](/wiki/United_States_at_the_2018_Winter_Paralympics "United States at the 2018 Winter Paralympics") (USA) | 5 | 5 | 3 | 13 |
| 2 | [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands_at_the_2018_Winter_Paralympics "Netherlands at the 2018 Winter Paralympics") (NED) | 2 | 2 | 1 | 5 |
| 3 | [Australia](/wiki/Australia_at_the_2018_Winter_Paralympics "Australia at the 2018 Winter Paralympics") (AUS) | 1 | 0 | 1 | 2 |
| [Finland](/wiki/Finland_at_the_2018_Winter_Paralympics "Finland at the 2018 Winter Paralympics") (FIN) | 1 | 0 | 1 | 2 |
| [Japan](/wiki/Japan_at_the_2018_Winter_Paralympics "Japan at the 2018 Winter Paralympics") (JPN) | 1 | 0 | 1 | 2 |
| 6 | [France](/wiki/France_at_the_2018_Winter_Paralympics "France at the 2018 Winter Paralympics") (FRA) | 0 | 1 | 1 | 2 |
| 7 | [Austria](/wiki/Austria_at_the_2018_Winter_Paralympics "Austria at the 2018 Winter Paralympics") (AUT) | 0 | 1 | 0 | 1 |

### Women's events

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Snowboarding_at_the_2018_Winter_Paralympics&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Women's events")]
